name: IRC_PURGE_HISTORY
description: The table is use to store history of recruiting purging job execution information for each entity

columns.Name: PURGE_HISTORY_ID
columns.Comments: Primary key of the table. System generated

columns.Name: ESS_REQUEST_ID
columns.Comments: The process id of the ESS job that purged the entity

columns.Name: ENTITY_ID
columns.Comments: This stores entity name that was purged.

columns.Name: ACTIVE_DAYS
columns.Comments: Number of active days kept in the entity.

columns.Name: BATCH_SIZE
columns.Comments: The number of rows to be purged in each batch for each entity type.

columns.Name: EXECUTION_ORDER
columns.Comments: The priority order of the entity that was purged.

columns.Name: ENTITIES_PURGED_COUNT
columns.Comments: The number of entities that were purged.

columns.Name: START_TIME
columns.Comments: Start time of the purge process for a entity.

columns.Name: END_TIME
columns.Comments: End time of the purge process for a entity.

columns.Name: STATUS
columns.Comments: This stores the status of the purge process e.g. IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELED, FAILED etc.

columns.Name: ERROR_CODE
columns.Comments: This is use to store error codes, which could be oracle error code or custom error codes.

columns.Name: ERROR_MESSAGE
columns.Comments: The error message returned by PL/SQL. This could be both oracle error message and custom error message.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.
